content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
· 428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329 
- (916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
.,.., 
Alan Seem 
Sheannan & Sterling LLP 
1460 El Camino Real, 2nd Floor 
Menlo Park, CA 94025 
Lori Liu, Mayor 
City of Brisbane 
50 Park Place 
Brisbane, CA 94005 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-17-103 
Dear Mr. Seem: 
April 28, 2017 
This letter responds to your request for advice as counsel to the China Silicon Valley 
Business Development ("CSV"), a California non-profit corporation, on behalf of Lori Liu, Mayor 
of the City of Brisbane, regarding the gift provisions of the Political Refonn Act (the "Act").1 Note 
that the Act imposes duties on public officials and on donors to the officials.2 
Please note that we are only providing advice under the gift and conflict of interest 
provisions of the Act and not under other general conflict of interest prohibitions such as common 
law conflict of interest or Section 1090. Additionally, the Fair Political Practices Commission (the 
'"Commission") does not act as a finder of fact when rendering advice (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 
FPPC Ops. 71), and this letter is based on the facts presented. 
QUESTION 
Are payments by the Chengdu Municipal People's Government for Mayor Liu's trip to 
China subject to the gift limits set forth in the Act? 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are lo the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 ofTitle 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 
? Section 89521 provides: "Any person who makes or receives an honorarium, gift, or expenditure in violation 
of this chapter is liable in a civil action brought by the Commission for an amount of up to three times the amount of the 
unlawful honorarium, gift, or expenditure. 


CONCLUSION 
File No. A -17-103 
Page No. 2 
The travel, lodging and meal payments from the Chengdu Municipal People's Government 
are reportable gifts that are not subject to the gift limits pursuant to Section 89506(a)(2). 
FACTS 
CSV has invited Mayor Liu to travel to China as part of the "May 2017 China Trip." You 
state that the purpose, sponsorship, and certain arrangements for this trip are similar to the Silicon 
Valley Mayors' China Trips previously organized by CSV, including the recent "March 2017 China 
Trip." We have issued letters regarding these trips and other China trips organized by CSV.3 
CSV's purpose in organizing this trip is to facilitate investment and international trade, and 
to promote communications between China and the Silicon Valley region. Mayor Liu is scheduled 
to arrive in China on May 8, 20 I 7 and visit Chengdu City, Sichuan Province. Mayor Liu returns on 
May 13, 2017. While in China, she will have the opportunity to participate in meetings with local 
government officials, potential investors and CEOs from local companies. Specifically, she will be 
attending the 2017 China Chengdu Global Innovation and Entrepreneurship Fair, and the 2017 
Chengdu International Sister Cities Innovation Forum, hosted by Chengdu Municipal People's 
Government. The invitation states that the visit will "increase international exchange and 
cooperation revolving around innovation and entrepreneurship, provide a platform for international 
sister cities and friendly cities to explore the latest trend, demonstrate achievements, and promote 
resource integration and project cooperation." You state that the "principal purpose of the May 
2017 China Trip is to continue to facilitate investment and international trade, and promote 
communications between China and the Silicon Valley region." You note that "the mayor will have 
opportunities to participate in meetings with local government officials, potential investors and 
CE Os from local companies." 
You state with respect to financing, that the Chengdu Municipal People's Government, a 
local Chinese government authority, will pay for the following estimated expenses for Mayor Liu: 
• All international air travel expenses to and from China; $2,000 per person 
• Remaining expenses for travel, lodging and meals within China; $3,000 per person 
You also state that "CSV will not provide any funds or services with monetary value to the 
mayor in connection with the May 2017 China Trip." 
ANALYSIS 
TJ,e Act's Gift Limit Provisio11s: 
A gift is defined under Section 82028(a) as: 
1 Advice Letter, Seem No. A-17-085; Informational Assistance Letters, Seem No. 1-16-236; No. 1-16-079 and 
No. 1-15-189, and collective requests for advice Reyes, et al Nos. A-14-071, A-14-072, A-14-078 and A-14-079. 


File No. A -17-103 
Page No. 3 
" . . .  any payment that confers a personal benefit on the recipient, to the extent that 
consideration of equal or greater value is not received and includes a rebate or 
discount in the price of anything of value unless the rebate or discount is made in 
the regular course of business to members of the public without regard to official 
status." 
To reduce improper influences on public officials, the Act regulates the receipt of gifts by 
local public officials in three ways: 
• Gift Limit of $470: The Act places limitations on the acceptance of gifts by certain public 
officials. The current limit is $470 from a single source in a calendar year. (Section 89503; 
Regulation 18940.2.) This gift limit applies to all public officials. (Sections 89503( a) and 
87200.) 
• Gift Reporting: So that the public is made aware of any potential influences from gifts, the 
Act imposes reporting obligations on certain public officials requiring that any gift (or any 
gifts that aggregate to $50 or more from the same source) received during the calendar year 
are disclosed on the officials' statements of economic interests. (Sections 87200 - 87210.) 
• Conflict of Interest Disqualification: The Act prohibits any public official from making, 
participating in making, or using his or her position to influence the outcome of a 
governmental decision involving the donor of a gift or gifts with an aggregate value of $470 
or more provided to, received by, or promised to the official within the 12 months prior to 
the date the decision is made. (Sections 87100 and 87103(e); Regulations 18700 and 
18703.4.) 
Under some circumstances, even though a payment for transportation, lodging, and 
subsistence may be a reportable gift under the Act, the gift is not subject to the Act's current $470 
gift limit. In this regard, Section 89506(a) provides (in pertinent part) an exception from the gift 
limits for certain travel payments: 
Payments, advances, or reimbursements, for travel, including actual 
transportation and related lodging and subsistence that is reasonably related to a 
legislative or governmental purpose, or to an issue of state, national, or 
international public policy, are not prohibited or limited by this chapter if either of 
the following apply: 
(1) .... 
(2) The travel is provided by a government, a governmental agency, a 
foreign government, a governmental authority, a bona fide public or private 
educational institution, as defined in Section 203 of the Revenue and Taxation 
Code, a nonprofit organization that is exempt from taxation under Section 
501 ( c )(3) of the Internal Revenue Code, or by a person domiciled outside the 
United States who substantially satisfies the requirements for tax - exempt status 
under Section 501(c)(3) of the Internal Revenue Code. 


File No. A -17-103 
Page No. 4 
Here, the travel is directly related to a legislative or governmental purpose and is provided 
by a foreign government. The stated purpose of this trip is to facilitate investment and international 
trade, and promote communications between China and the Silicon Valley region. While in China, 
Mayor Liu will have opportunities to participate in meetings with local government officials, 
potential investors, and CEOs from local companies. Chengdu Municipal People's Government will 
pay for all international air travel expenses to and from China, as well as remaining expenses for 
travel, lodging and meals within China during the trip. Consequently, the specified payments are 
reportable gifts that are not subject to gift limits pursuant to Section 89506(a)(2). 4 We discuss the 
conflict of interest issues further below. 
Please note that any payments made for items other than travel and related lodging and 
subsistence may be considered a reportable gift, subject to the $470 gift limit, and subject to the 
conflict of interest provisions of the Act. For all gifts, if the official reimburses the donor or 
donor's intermediary for the gift within 30 days, no gift is considered to have been received or 
accepted. (Regulation 18941 (c)(3).) 
Conflict of Interest Disqualification 
As mentioned above, a public official who receives gift(s) of $470 or more may have a 
financial conflict of interest under the Act. (Sections 87100 et seq.) The official must disqualify 
himself or herself from voting or otherwise participating in a governmental decision affecting that 
source, if the payment was received or promised to the official within 12 months preceding the 
decision. Therefore, if the public official accepts gifts valued at $470 or more, the official may be 
prohibited from participating in governmental decisions affecting the source of those gifts. 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
ZWN:jgl 
Sincerely, 
Hyla P. Wagner 
General Counsel 
3.r"fJk-
By: 
Zachary W. Norton 
Senior Counsel, Legal Division 
4 Note that Regulation l 8945(a) provides: "The person who makes the gift to the official(s) is the source of the 
gift unless that person is acting as an intennediary. The person is acting as an intermediary for the source of the gift 
when the gift to the official was provided under any of the following conditions: (1) the person receives a payment from 
a source and the payment is made to the official after the source identifies the official as the intended recipient of the 
gift; (2) the person receives a payment from a source after soliciting the payment with the understanding that the 
payment will be used for the sole or primary purpose of making a gift to an official; or (3) the person receives a 
payment from a source after the payment was solicited by the official or the official's agent for the purpose of making a 
gift to the official."

File No. A -17-103  
The travel, lodging and meal payments from the Chengdu Municipal People's Government 
are reportable gifts that are not subject to the gift limits pursuant to Section 89506(a)(2).

QUESTION: Are payments by the Chengdu Municipal People's Government for Mayor Liu's trip to 
China subject to the gift limits set forth in the Act?? Section 89521 provides: "Any person who makes or receives an honorarium, gift, or expenditure in violation 
of this chapter is liable in a civil action brought by the Commission for an amount of up to three times the amount of the 
unlawful honorarium, gift, or expenditure.

CONCLUSION: File No. A -17-103  
The travel, lodging and meal payments from the Chengdu Municipal People's Government 
are reportable gifts that are not subject to the gift limits pursuant to Section 89506(a)(2).
